title: <div>  3.  Do we need a way for students to “earn back” participation if they do
content:
3. Do we need a way for students to “earn back” participation if they do the challenge mode but don’t score highly?
4. Should we create a short video or guide that introduces the research paper & creative components ahead of Week 11?

Once these are answered, I’ll finalize everything for the locked-in syllabus. 🚀

📌 Updated & Expanded Protocols Based on Your Notes


I’ve incorporated everything you suggested, adding multiple student paths, adjusting the catharsis/reflection spaces, refining the final project options, and addressing the final lock-in questions with solutions. Here’s the complete breakdown:

1️⃣ Multiple Paths to Completion (Flexible Course Structure)

We now have three distinct pathways for students to complete the course, allowing for flexibility based on their strengths and learning styles.

📜 Path A: The In-Class Engagement Path

✔ Heavy participation in in-class discussions, activities, and collaborative work.
context: From document: Comprehensive Course Structure and Alternating Schedule (A & B Weeks… — chunk 42 (chunk 42/108)
